# Sample Theory Paper

## 1. Introduction

This synthetic file exists only to test deterministic Markdown preparation.
It is not intended to represent a complete economic model.
The paper studies a principal who delegates a task to an agent.
The agent chooses effort after accepting a contract.
Effort is not observed by the principal.
Output is informative about effort but also contains noise.

## 2. Model

There is one risk-neutral principal and one risk-averse agent.
The principal offers an output-contingent wage schedule.
The agent accepts or rejects the contract.
After acceptance, the agent privately selects effort.
Output is then realized and the wage is paid.
The agent dislikes effort and values expected compensation.
The principal values output net of compensation.
The participation constraint ensures that the agent prefers participation.
The incentive constraint ensures that chosen effort is optimal for the agent.

## 3. Result

A stronger output signal permits stronger incentives.
Greater agent risk aversion makes incentive provision more costly.
The second-best contract therefore balances incentives and risk sharing.

## 4. Welfare

The synthetic example does not provide a formal welfare theorem.
It merely illustrates the source-preparation workflow.
